compiled_state.title: Rename using-lockpick to using-dso and add sub-agent delegation guidance
compiled_state.description:
## Context
DSO plugin orchestrators and sprint sub-agents currently lack startup-hook guidance on Agent tool delegation. The using-lockpick startup hook — injected into every conversation — establishes discipline around skill invocation but contains zero guidance about when to delegate work to sub-agents. As a result, Claude defaults to doing all work inline: reading files sequentially, editing serially, and self-reviewing its own output, leading to longer sprint wall-clock times and more frequent context-window exhaustion. The skill also carries a legacy name (using-lockpick) that no longer reflects the broader plugin identity (dso); the rename is cosmetic hygiene bundled here because it touches the same file.

## Success Criteria
1. The sub-agent guidance section explicitly identifies sequential multi-file reads (10+ files) as a Red Flag and prescribes Explore agent delegation as the recommended behavior
2. The sub-agent guidance section explicitly identifies serial independent file edits as a Red Flag and prescribes parallel coding agent delegation as the recommended behavior
3. The sub-agent guidance section explicitly identifies inline self-review as a Red Flag and prescribes dedicated reviewer agent delegation as the recommended behavior (generic delegation pattern — must not name specific reviewer agents or mirror dso-8l5h implementation details)
4. The skill is named using-dso everywhere — no references to using-lockpick remain in active code, config, skill files, tests, or CLAUDE.md (exempt: CHANGELOG.md entries and git commit messages)
5. The sub-agent guidance follows the Red Flags table pattern: a two-column table with columns Thought (the rationalization) and Reality (why delegation is better), consistent with the existing skill invocation Red Flags table in the same file
6. The sub-agent guidance section does not exceed 60 rendered lines in the skill file, inclusive of blank lines and headers
7. The renamed startup hook (using-dso) fires successfully in the test suite, and the skill eval suite validates that the sub-agent Red Flags table content is present and well-formed

Scope boundary: This epic covers policy-level startup-hook guidance only. Task decomposition mechanics (2e8e-72d9) and token budgeting (dso-zj3r) are out of scope.

## Scenario Analysis
- Partial rename leaves using-lockpick references active in hook dispatcher (critical, Deployment): If the hook dispatcher still references using-lockpick, the using-dso content is never injected.
- Skill registry key collision or missing entry (critical, Deployment): Window where startup hook is registered under neither name.
- Test suite references using-lockpick by hardcoded name (high, Deployment): Tests fail after rename due to hardcoded strings.
- CLAUDE.md reference update missed (high, Deployment): Agents invoke a non-existent skill name.
- Recursive agent spawning on ambiguous tasks (high, Runtime): Sub-agent delegation guidance could trigger unbounded agent nesting.
- Guidance conflicts with existing Never-Do rules (medium, Runtime): Oscillation between startup hook guidance and CLAUDE.md rules.
- Host projects with using-lockpick pinned in config (high, Configuration): Upgrading silently disables startup hook.
- Skill eval references using-lockpick (medium, Configuration): Eval suite tests a non-existent skill name.

## Dependencies
- dso-t7af (soft): Removes the 5-sub-agent cap — guidance focuses on when to delegate rather than cap mechanics. If dso-t7af merges after this epic, revisit guidance for consistency.
- dso-9fgn (soft): Limits tool use by sub-agents — guidance avoids prescribing tool allowlists
- dso-8l5h (soft): Extracts review sub-agents — SC 3 references generic delegation patterns

## Approach
Rename using-lockpick to using-dso (clean break, no alias — host projects must update config manually) and add a Sub-Agent Delegation section with a Red Flags rationalization table scoped to policy-level delegation principles. Update all references across hooks, tests, configs, CLAUDE.md, and the skill registry.

### Planning Intelligence Log
- Web research (Step 2.6): not triggered
  - Bright-line conditions that fired: none
- Scenario analysis (Step 2.75): triggered
  - Scenarios surviving blue team filter: 8
- Practitioner-requested additional cycles: none

comments.body: Adversarial review: 7 findings, 7 accepted. Full exchange: /tmp/workflow-plugin-5e951c5e86faa933/adversarial-review-rename-using-dso.json
